title: Secure first confirmed adoption partner
context: No adoption partner has been secured yet and all tasks carry verifiedNeed:false. The project's 'delivered, not merged' bar requires datasets to be adopted into real teaching before they count as shipped outcomes. Securing a first named partner (an educator, course, or workshop that commits to using a dataset in real teaching) is a key M1 exit criterion and unlocks setting verifiedNeed:true on partner-specific tasks. The outreach-007 task opened threads; this task converts a thread into a confirmed commitment. Depends on outreach-007.
objective: Secure a named first adoption partner — an educator or program who confirms they will use at least one project dataset in real teaching — and document the adoption mechanism and commitment.
acceptanceCriteria: A named educator/program confirms in writing they will use a specific dataset in real teaching (course, workshop, or curriculum inclusion).
Adoption mechanism documented: which dataset, which lesson/course, which platform (lesson repo PR / platform / syllabus inclusion), and expected learner count.
Tasks for that partner updated to verifiedNeed:true with requestor set to the confirmed partner.
Steward records the adoption-evidence artifact (outcomes/<dataset-id>.json or equivalent) with the syllabus/workshop URL or written educator confirmation.
Document committed to the project repo; commit DCO signed-off.
resources: open-teaching-datasets-pilot-008 (the dataset most likely to attract an early partner)
output: A committed document recording the first confirmed adoption partner, the adoption mechanism, and a link to the outcomes evidence artifact, unblocking verifiedNeed:true on partner-specific tasks.
